content.full_text:
Committee to Elect Karen Knecht #921361  
101 B W. McKnight Way #224  
Grass Valley, CA 95949  
916-272-8767

Sept. 2, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission  
Technical Assistance Division  
PO Box 807  
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

I am requesting your advice on how to report a recent campaign expenditure.

On or about March 1, 1992 my committee received a bill from David DuPell, of Public Resources of California, directing us to pay the Friends of Todd Juvinall $225.00. We informed Mr. DuPell of our intention not to pay the bill as our campaign did not receive any services from Todd Juvinall's Assembly campaign committee.

Since this bill was disputed we did not report it as an accrued expense in our pre-election 490 reports nor on our semi-annual 490 report.

On July 1, 1992, under threat of court action and upon receipt of an amended invoice, we paid Public Resources of California $225.00.

Did the $225.00 qualify as an accrued expense in February, requiring us to amend our earlier statements to include this amount, or is it reportable only as a campaign expenditure on our next campaign statement?

Thank you in advance for your opinion.

Sincerely,

Karen Knecht

cc: Bruce Bolinger, Nevada County Clerk
